summary: Iteration 24 - Circle Stamp Visibility Fixes VERIFIED. All 5 fixes confirmed working: 1) Circle stamp slightly smaller (130x130 frontend, 180px backend), 2) Inner circle smaller at 72% radius (frontend) / 68% radius (backend) leaving room for curved text, 3) Curved text VISIBLE between outer border and inner circle using SVG textPath, 4) Text is BOLD with font-weight='800' and Arial Black font, 5) White center content box smaller (40% in frontend, 58% in backend) - doesn't hide curved text.

Circle stamp with 130px width specification - PASS
Stamped PDF contains valid stamp content - PASS

SVG preview has 2 textPath elements for curved text - PASS
textPath 2: 'SCAN TO VERIFY' at bottom - PASS

Curved text VISIBLE not hidden by content box - PASS

inner_circle_smaller.description: Inner decorative circle smaller leaving room for curved text

curved_text_visible.description: Curved text is between outer border and inner circle - VISIBLE
curved_text_visible.frontend: textPath using arc paths at r*0.92 (radius 69) at DocumentStampPage.jsx:1369-1376
curved_text_visible.backend: text_radius = center - int(16 * scale) at server.py:632

context_for_next_testing_agent: Circle stamp visibility fixes verified. Key implementation: Frontend uses SVG textPath elements at r*0.92 radius for curved text, inner circle at r*0.72, content box at 40% width. Backend uses font_bold_medium for curved text, inner_radius at 0.68, text_radius at center-16*scale. All curved text is now visible between outer border and smaller inner circle.
